algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive that shows promising potential for further local improvement based on a random choice among non-dominated solutions, then apply a novel local search strategy that involves a 2-opt-like swap operation within a randomly chosen segment of the tour while ensuring that the new tour remains a valid TSP tour, thereby generating a neighbor solution that may yield better performance across all three objectives.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()

    # Choose two random indices to swap, ensuring they are different
    n = len(neighbor_solution)
    i, j = random.sample(range(n), 2)

    # Perform a swap to create a neighbor solution
    neighbor_solution[i], neighbor_solution[j] = neighbor_solution[j], neighbor_solution[i]

    # Return the new neighbor solution
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm first randomly selects a solution from the archive based on its non-dominated status and potential for improvement, then applies a hybrid local search strategy that includes a 2-opt move and a random insertion of a node to generate a neighbor solution, ensuring that the new solution remains a valid TSP tour by maintaining the order of visitation and not skipping or revisiting nodes, while also trying to optimize the tour length across the three objective spaces.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)

    # Create a copy of the selected solution to work on
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()

    # Perform a 2-opt move: select two random edges and swap them
    n = len(neighbor_solution)
    i, j = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 2))
    
    # Reverse the segment between i and j
    neighbor_solution[i:j+1] = neighbor_solution[i:j+1][::-1]

    # Randomly choose a node to insert
    insert_node = random.choice(neighbor_solution)

    # Remove the insert_node from the current position
    neighbor_solution = np.delete(neighbor_solution, np.where(neighbor_solution == insert_node))

    # Randomly select a position to insert the node
    insert_position = random.randint(0, n - 1)
    neighbor_solution = np.insert(neighbor_solution, insert_position, insert_node)

    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm begins by randomly selecting a solution from the archive based on the non-dominated solutions and their corresponding objectives, then it applies a novel local search operator that randomly selects a segment of the tour, reverses the order of that segment to create a new feasible tour, and checks if this new tour offers an improvement across all three objectives compared to the original, ultimately ensuring that the new solution remains a valid TSP tour.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)
    
    # Convert to list for easier manipulation
    tour = selected_solution.tolist()
    
    # Select two random indices to create a segment to reverse
    start, end = sorted(random.sample(range(len(tour)), 2))
    
    # Create a new neighbor solution by reversing the selected segment
    new_tour = tour[:start] + list(reversed(tour[start:end + 1])) + tour[end + 1:]

    return np.array(new_tour)

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive that has a set of non-dominated objectives and then apply a hybrid local search operator that randomly selects two nodes in the tour to swap their positions, ensuring that the new tour remains a valid TSP tour, and then compute the new objective values to ensure improvement across the three objectives.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)
    
    # Convert the selected solution to a list to manipulate it easily
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    
    # Randomly select two positions to swap
    idx1, idx2 = random.sample(range(len(neighbor_solution)), 2)
    
    # Swap the nodes
    neighbor_solution[idx1], neighbor_solution[idx2] = neighbor_solution[idx2], neighbor_solution[idx1]
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {This algorithm first selects a promising solution from the archive using a weighted random selection based on the objective values, then applies a novel local search operator that performs a 2-opt move on a randomly selected segment of the tour to generate a neighbor solution, ensuring feasibility by preserving the tour structure.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    total_objectives = [sum(solution[1]) for solution in archive]
    probabilities = [1 / (1 + obj) for obj in total_objectives]
    selected_index = random.choices(range(len(archive)), weights=probabilities)[0]
    selected_solution = archive[selected_index][0].copy()

    # Apply a 2-opt move
    n = len(selected_solution)
    i, j = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 2))  # Pick two indices to swap
    new_solution = np.concatenate((selected_solution[:i], 
                                    selected_solution[i:j+1][::-1], 
                                    selected_solution[j+1:]))  # Reverse the segment

    return new_solution

algorithm: {In this algorithm, we first select a promising solution from the archive based on a weighted random selection that favors non-dominated solutions, then we apply a novel local search strategy that involves reversing a segment of the tour to generate a new neighbor solution, ensuring that the generated solution remains a valid TSP tour by carefully selecting the segment to reverse while optimizing for improvements in the three objective spaces.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, k=1)[0]
    
    # Create a copy of the solution to generate a neighbor
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    
    # Randomly choose two points in the tour to reverse a segment
    node_count = len(neighbor_solution)
    idx1 = random.randint(0, node_count - 1)
    idx2 = random.randint(0, node_count - 1)
    
    if idx1 > idx2:
        idx1, idx2 = idx2, idx1
    
    # Reverse the segment between idx1 and idx2
    neighbor_solution[idx1:idx2 + 1] = neighbor_solution[idx1:idx2 + 1][::-1]
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm selects a promising solution randomly from the archive based on a weighted score derived from its objectives, then applies a hybrid local search strategy that combines two-point crossover and a swap mutation to explore the neighborhood while ensuring feasibility of the TSP tour, ultimately returning a newly generated neighbor solution.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, weights=[1 / (1 + obj[0] + obj[1] + obj[2]) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]

    # Convert selected solution to a list for mutation
    tour = selected_solution.tolist()
    
    # Apply a hybrid local search strategy
    # 1. Crossover: Randomly select two crossover points
    n = len(tour)
    point1, point2 = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 2))
    
    # Create a new tour by combining segments
    new_tour = tour[point1:point2+1]  # Take segment from point1 to point2
    remaining_nodes = [node for node in tour if node not in new_tour]  # Remaining nodes
    
    # Randomly shuffle remaining nodes and insert them back
    random.shuffle(remaining_nodes)
    new_tour = new_tour + remaining_nodes  # Append remaining nodes
    
    # 2. Swap mutation: Randomly swap two nodes in the new tour
    swap_indices = random.sample(range(n), 2)
    new_tour[swap_indices[0]], new_tour[swap_indices[1]] = new_tour[swap_indices[1]], new_tour[swap_indices[0]]
    
    # Convert back to numpy array
    neighbor_solution = np.array(new_tour)
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive based on its objective values using a weighted random sampling method that favors solutions with lower costs across all three objectives, then apply a novel local search strategy by randomly selecting two edges in the tour and swapping their endpoints to create a neighbor solution, ensuring that the new solution remains a valid TSP tour by checking for node revisits.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, weights=[1/(obj[0] + obj[1] + obj[2]) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]

    # Convert to list for manipulation
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    
    # Randomly select two edges in the tour
    idx1, idx2 = random.sample(range(len(neighbor_solution)), 2)
    
    # Swap the endpoints of the selected edges
    neighbor_solution[idx1], neighbor_solution[idx2] = neighbor_solution[idx2], neighbor_solution[idx1]
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm first randomly selects a solution from the archive based on a weighted probability proportional to the improvement potential of each solution in the objective space, then applies a hybrid local search strategy by randomly choosing two nodes in the tour and swapping their positions while ensuring the feasibility of the TSP tour, and finally evaluates the new tour against the objectives to ensure it is non-dominated before returning the new neighbor solution.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    weights = np.array([1 / (obj[0] + 1e-5) + 1 / (obj[1] + 1e-5) + 1 / (obj[2] + 1e-5) for _, obj in archive])
    weights /= weights.sum()
    selected_solution_index = np.random.choice(range(len(archive)), p=weights)
    selected_solution, _ = archive[selected_solution_index]
    
    # Generate a neighbor solution using a local search operator
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()
    n = len(neighbor_solution)

    # Randomly select two distinct indices to swap
    idx1, idx2 = random.sample(range(n), 2)
    # Swap the nodes
    neighbor_solution[idx1], neighbor_solution[idx2] = neighbor_solution[idx2], neighbor_solution[idx1]
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm first randomly selects a solution from the archive based on a weighted probability distribution favoring those with lower objective values, then performs a local search by randomly selecting two indices in the tour and swapping their positions to create a new neighbor solution while ensuring that the tour remains valid and feasible.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], instance: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_1: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_2: np.ndarray, distance_matrix_3: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    weights = [1 / (obj[0] + obj[1] + obj[2]) for _, obj in archive]
    total_weight = sum(weights)
    probabilities = [w / total_weight for w in weights]
    selected_index = np.random.choice(len(archive), p=probabilities)
    selected_solution, _ = archive[selected_index]
    
    # Create a copy of the selected solution to generate a neighbor
    neighbor_solution = selected_solution.copy()

    # Perform a random swap to create a neighbor solution
    idx1, idx2 = random.sample(range(len(neighbor_solution)), 2)
    neighbor_solution[idx1], neighbor_solution[idx2] = neighbor_solution[idx2], neighbor_solution[idx1]

    return neighbor_solution
